codeReview.summary: The fix does add the missing VAL-TYPES-005 unit coverage by registering a dedicated JSON-RPC guard suite for request, notification, and response messages, but the feature still fails review because its own commit repeats unrelated release-version changes and the new test file exceeds the mission's 200-line file limit.

issues.description: This test-coverage fix commit again bumps the package version (0.5.9 -> 0.5.10) even though the feature is only about adding JSON-RPC guard tests. The original scrutiny failure already called out unrelated version metadata changes, so this fix does not cleanly resolve that part of the failure.
The Chrome extension manifest version is also bumped as part of this test-only feature. That is an unrelated release-scoped side effect outside the fix feature's scope.

The Firefox manifest version is bumped in the same commit even though the feature only needs to add JSON-RPC unit tests. This repeats the same class of unrelated metadata change previously flagged on the original feature.

The new JSON-RPC test suite is 285 lines long, which violates the mission and refactor-worker requirement that affected files stay at or under 200 lines. The added coverage is useful, but the feature still misses the mission's hard file-size constraint.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The refactor-worker procedure and the worker's own handoff status are out of sync: this fix records `followedProcedure: true` even though the transcript/handoff evidence does not show the full required final validation sequence being completed successfully before commit.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:90-107 requires final validation to include lint, check:repo-standards, and build; the fix handoff only records `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `wc -l`, then marks `followedProcedure: true` in /Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/handoffs/2026-03-11T00-57-39-403Z__fix-json-rpc-test-coverage__6d28002a-fc61-4872-98ae-160c30b472cf.json (lines 16-28, 81).

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission's file-size rule is inconsistently represented in shared state: mission guidance and the refactor-worker skill require files to stay under 200 lines, but workers are still relying on a 300-line threshold for tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: /Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/AGENTS.md:13,39 and .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:77-80 set a 200-line hard limit, but the fix handoff explicitly says `Test file is 235 lines, well under the 300 line limit` and cites a `300 line limit` again for runtime-messages.test.ts in the same handoff file (lines 28, 77).

summary: Review failed. The fix supplies the missing JSON-RPC type-guard coverage that the original feature lacked, but it still repeats unrelated version bumps and adds a 285-line test file, so it does not cleanly resolve the original scrutiny failure.
